meaning: n.用户至上主义,商品的消费和销售性服务
examples: They have clearly embraced Western consumerism.
Turns out, according to Daniel Cook, a historian of childhood consumerism, it was popularised as a marketing trick by clothing manufacturers in the 1930s.
Or so at least I tell myself now as I prepare for the raucous consumerism of downtown.
During the past 40 years, consumerism has become a central part of American life.
